data.title: Syntheia signs non-binding LOI to acquire SATCO
data.description:
• Syntheia (SYAIF) said on Monday it has entered a non-binding LOI with CX1 Capital to acquire Wunderlich Group LLC, dba Satcom.
  • The proposed acquisition is expected to be valued at $5.5M, comprising a $1.45M cash payment on closing, $2.22M in promissory notes, and $1.83M in Syntheia common shares issued at a 90-day volume-weighted average price.
  • Additionally, the company will also pay up to $4.5M in a performance-based earn-out to Satcom, to be settled through a combination of cash, promissory notes, and common shares.

data.title: Gold, silver surge to record highs on fears of increased Fed interference, safe-haven demand
data.description:
Gold and silver futures jumped to fresh new records after the Trump administration's criminal probe into Federal Reserve Chair Powell added to concerns about the independence of the U.S. central bank, while heightened civil unrest in Iran that reportedly has killed hundreds of protesters drove safe-haven demand to the precious metals markets.

Powell said Trump's threat to indict him over his testimony before the U.S. Senate Banking Committee in June regarding the Fed's $2.5B renovation of two office buildings - which Trump has criticized as excessive - is a "pretext" to gain control over interest rate cuts that Trump has long sought.

Trump's latest move - which he said was launched by the Department of Justice without his knowledge - "again speaks not only to the ridiculously volatile nature of the Trump Admin, but will also again shake confidence in U.S. economic institutions, and by extension assets," Pepperstone senior research strategist Michael Brown said in a note.

Trump also said he is weighing potential options in response to reports of the deadly crackdowns in Iran, but added that Iran's leadership has reached out to seek talks.

"The ousting of [Venezuela's] Maduro is supportive of higher gold prices in our view, given the short and long-term uncertainty it introduces for commodity markets and for Washington's relations with Beijing and Moscow," BMI analysts saod in a note. "Additionally, our Political Risk team now sees heightened risks of U.S. action against Iran in early 2026 should the current protests escalate."

Front-month Comex gold (XAUUSD:CUR) for January delivery closed +2.5% to $4,604.30/oz, settling above $4,600 for the first time, and front-month Comex January silver (XAGUSD:CUR) ended +7.2% to $84.610/oz.

ETFs: (GLD), (GDX), (GDXJ), (IAU), (NUGT), (PHYS), (GLDM), (AAAU), (SGOL), (RING), (BAR), (OUNZ), (SLV), (PSLV), (SIVR), (SIL), (SILJ)

data.title: U.S. Dollar slips amid political developments putting focus on Fed: Currency Recap
data.description:
The U.S. dollar eased on Monday, slipping as investors reacted to political developments in Washington and renewed focus on Federal Reserve independence.

The greenback was last down 0.24% at $98.89, although the dollar index remains up 0.66% so far this year.

Dollar weekly moves and key drivers:

The U.S. Dollar Index (DXY), which measures the dollar against a basket of six major currencies, rose 0.81% over the past week, even as sentiment turned cautious at the start of the new trading week.

 

Dollar weakness followed reports that U.S. prosecutors had opened an investigation involving Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell, prompting a knee-jerk drop in the currency during Asian and European trade. The developments sparked debate around political pressure on the central bank, with several U.S. lawmakers publicly backing the Fed’s independence.

Bank of America commentary was also closely watched by currency markets over the week. The bank said it expects the euro to strengthen in 2026, citing lower U.S. interest rates and improved conditions in China, while also advising clients to consider closing long USD/JPY positions as the pair approaches 160. Separate BofA research pointed to the risk of further dollar weakness ahead, noting that large dollar selloffs have historically tended to extend into consecutive years.

Elsewhere, geopolitical developments tied to Venezuela continued to influence currency markets through their impact on oil prices. Earlier in the week, declines in oil weighed on commodity-linked currencies such as the Canadian dollar and Norwegian krone, while broader FX moves remained contained.

In Asia, currencies were mostly subdued. The dollar slipped against regional peers after reports linked to the Powell investigation, while the Chinese yuan held near recent strength following firm inflation data, even as analysts warned of potential near-term pullbacks.

Markets were also cautious ahead of key U.S. economic data releases, with trading conditions remaining muted as investors assessed political risks and the outlook for U.S. policy.

U.S. Treasury yields were mixed over the week. The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) rose two points to 4.19%, while the 2-year yield (US2Y) climbed eight points to 3.55%.

Currency Highlights:

Major currency movements (January 05 to January 12)

Euro (EUR:USD) -0.56%
Pound Sterling (GBP:USD) -0.88%
Japanese Yen (JPY:USD) -1.25%
Chinese Yuan (CNY:USD) +0.15%
Swiss Franc (CHF:USD) -0.86%
Australian Dollar (AUD:USD) -0.28%
Canadian Dollar (CAD:USD) -0.87%

The Japanese yen (JPY:USD) was last down 0.22%, even as Asian equity markets were mostly higher, while Japan’s finance minister reiterated readiness to intervene in currency markets if needed.

The British pound (GBP:USD) was last up 0.46%, as UK data showed the composite and services PMI edging higher in December, while European equity markets, including the FTSE, closed at record highs during the week.

The Canadian dollar (CAD:USD) was last up 0.25%, recovering modestly after earlier weakness linked to oil price moves following developments tied to Venezuela earlier in the week.

The Euro (EUR:USD) was last up 0.27%, even as it remained lower over the week, with European equities hitting record highs and data showing firmer retail sales and lower unemployment, while inflation eased across the euro area in December.

Market Reactions and analysis:

An analyst pointed out that the dollar could come under pressure, adding that “we expect the dollar, bonds and stocks to all fall in Monday trading in a sell-America trade,” while an analyst at RBC Capital Markets said markets could begin to price in higher risk premiums, noting that “markets will start to price in greater inflation expectations, inflation risk premium, and term premium if the Fed’s independence comes under further attack.”

data.title: SPARC AI names Kartika Saran as CFO
data.description:
• SPARC AI (SPAIF) announced on Monday the appointment of Ms. Kartika Saran, CPA, CA, as CFO, effective immediately, succeeding John Dinan, who has stepped down from the role.
  • With nearly 20 years of experience in financial leadership, capital markets, and strategic finance, Saran currently serves as an audit partner at SHIM & Associates, Chartered Professional Accountants.
  • Shares +6.71%.

data.title: Trump says U.S. to impose 25% tariff on nations 'doing business' with Iran
data.description:
U.S. President Donald Trump said Monday that he is imposing a 25% tariff on goods from any country "doing business" with Iran, without offering details on how broadly the new duties would be applied.

“Effective immediately, any Country doing business with the Islamic Republic of Iran will pay a Tariff of 25% on any and all business being done with the United States of America. This Order is final and conclusive,” Trump said in a post on his social media platform Truth Social.

This is a developing story. Check back for updates.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Chipotle reaffirms FY25 guidance, announces executive changes
data.description:
Shares of Chipotle Mexican Grill (CMG) are under pressure in afterhours trading as the company  announced several management changes and reaffirmed its outlook for comparable restaurant sales to decline in the low-single digit range.

At the same time, the company announced that executive officers Chris Brandt and Roger Theodoredis have “transitioned” out of their roles effective January 12, remaining for a limited period as non-executive employees.

As Chipotle (CMG) conducts a search for a new chief marketing officer, Stephanie Perdue, the current vice president of brand marketing, will serve as CMO on an interim basis.

Additionally, Chief Human Resources Officer Ilene Eskenazi has been appointed as chief legal and human resources officer to succeed Chief Legal Officer and General Counsel Roger Theodoredis.

By remaining with the company for a limited period, Brandt and Theodoredis will ensure an orderly handover rather than a disruptive separation. Both will continue to receive their base salary and benefits, but will not receive new equity award grands.

data.title: Jamf appoints David Helfer as chief revenue officer
data.description:
• Jamf (JAMF) announced on Monday the appointment of David Helfer as chief revenue officer ((CRO)), effective immediately.
  • The company said that Helfer, in his role as CRO, will lead Jamf’s sales organization and help advance the company’s near- and long-term growth strategy.
  • Most recently,  Helfer served as chief customer and revenue officer at Mimecast, and before that, he held senior leadership roles at GitLab and F5.
  • Helfer will succeed Liz Benz, Jamf’s current chief sales officer, to build on the strong foundation built during her tenure, the company said.
  • JAMF -0.07% after hours to $13.01.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Travere Therapeutics provides corporate update and 2026 outlook
data.description:
• Travere Therapeutics (TVTX) said  on Monday it expects U.S. preliminary net product sales for Q4 2025 to be about $127M.
  • The company expects FY25 U.S. net product sales of about $410M, and the year ended with roughly $323M in cash and marketable securities.
  • The company also provided updates on key corporate, clinical, and regulatory initiatives, including expected milestones for 2026.
  • During the quarter the company received 908 new patient start forms.
  • Preliminary U.S. net product sales of FILSPARI were about $103M in Q4 2025, up 108% YoY, and approximately $322M for the full year.
  • In October 2025, the company received a $40M milestone payment from its collaborator CSL Vifor, following market access achievements.
  • In 2026, the company expects to advance clinical evidence supporting FILSPARI in IgAN and said its partner Chugai plans to submit a New Drug Application for sparsentan in Japan.
  • The PDUFA target action date for the sNDA seeking full approval of FILSPARI in FSGS is January 13, 2026.
  • Phase 3 HARMONY Study set to restart in Q1 2026 after 2025 manufacturing process improvements.

data.description:
